messages.content: Improve text "There were previous concerns over the size of the new proposed annex. We have sought to address this by reducing the length and reviewing the massing and roof lines. This was done by taking away the mass of the pitched roof and dropping the eaves line to single storey. This not only makes the building shorter it feels in proportion with the existing building. "

Previous concerns were raised regarding the size of the newly proposed annex. To tackle this, we have made significant improvements by reducing its length and carefully reassessing the overall massing and roof lines. Specifically, we removed the pitched roof, resulting in a more streamlined appearance. Additionally, we lowered the eaves line to a single storey level. Consequently, these modifications not only make the building shorter but also ensure it harmoniously complements the existing structure in terms of proportion and aesthetics.

Improve text "We were asked to consider having the annex physically connected to the existing. There were fears by the council that the property would be sold separately.  The current proposal does not achieve this connection. The reason is that the site reduces in width at the only location possible for the link. This results in an unacceptable proposal to our client as it is was significantly detrimental to the open landscape scheme being established to the ground as a whole. 
It is hoped that should this issue remain contentious that provision for the preservation of the site as a whole can be resolved through appropriate consent clauses. Our client appreciates the importance of maintaining the rural nature of the approaching road and surrounding area. "

We received a request to explore the possibility of physically connecting the annex to the existing structure. The council expressed concerns about the potential separation of the properties if they were sold separately. Regrettably, the current proposal does not fulfill this requirement. The main obstacle to achieving the connection is the narrowing of the site at the only feasible location for the link. This would result in a highly undesirable outcome for our client, as it would significantly compromise the envisioned open landscape scheme for the entire grounds.

However, we hope that if this issue continues to be a point of contention, a resolution can be reached by incorporating appropriate consent clauses that guarantee the preservation of the site as a whole. Our client is fully aware of and respects the importance of maintaining the rural character of the surrounding area, particularly with regards to the approach road.

Improve text "Top Image shows the previous design 
The Bottom image shows the redesigned scheme where the mass has been reduced lowering the roof line so that it is more subservient to the existing building. The look will be for an more modern barn style but the aesthetic remains."

The top image depicts the previous design, while the bottom image showcases the redesigned scheme. In the revised version, considerable efforts have been made to reduce the mass and lower the roof line, ensuring a more subordinate appearance in relation to the existing building. While the overall aesthetic has transitioned towards a more modern barn style, the essence and visual appeal of the structure are still intact.

Concept proposal drawn to investigate the possibility of connecting the new build with the existing. Linking the two unacceptably separated the front and rear gardens due to the restrictions of the location of the protected tree

A concept proposal has been prepared to explore the feasibility of connecting the new build with the existing structure. However, upon closer examination, it became clear that linking the two would result in an unacceptable separation of the front and rear gardens. This issue arises due to the presence and location restrictions imposed by a protected tree on the site.

Security along the site entrance has not been compromised as we are not proposing to remove existing hedges and trees that currently provide privacy, and the existing secure entrance gate and perimeter fence have been retained.
The annex is visible from the existing house. There are few openings on the Ground Floor, with secured garage doors provided at this level. Windows are located on the First Floor, providing views from the residential units over the adjacent private farm land, this allows for natural surveillance of the grounds and the surrounding area.
1800mm high wire fences are now present around the full boundary, and new hedges are becoming established to the West boundary with the adjacent farmland. This is in line with the local established character.

It is important to note that the security of the site entrance remains uncompromised in our proposal. We have made sure to preserve the existing hedges and trees that currently provide privacy. Additionally, the secure entrance gate and perimeter fence have been retained to ensure adequate security measures.

Regarding the visibility of the annex from the existing house, we have considered this aspect as well. The Ground Floor of the annex features minimal openings, primarily consisting of secured garage doors. On the First Floor, windows have been strategically positioned to offer views from the residential units overlooking the adjacent private farmland. This design allows for natural surveillance of the grounds and the surrounding area.

To further enhance security, 1800mm high wire fences have been installed around the entire boundary. In addition, new hedges have been planted along the West boundary, which aligns with the established character of the local area. These measures contribute to maintaining security and privacy throughout the site.

The site plan proposal locates the new annex centrally within the new section of ground previously farmland and newly changed to garden lawn. This setting is in keeping with the local character as it sets the proposal back from the road and behind the line of the existing main house. The perimeter planting will significantly obsure the building and provids privacy and security. This location also gives the client the best ability to provide inviting landscape around the property. 
The setting also works to achieve the characteristics of a modern barn conversion suitable for the rural area. There is a strong benefit in achieving the new accommodation separatly as it preserves the sacle of the existing building and over all character of the area a combined building would be two dominating.

The proposed site plan places the new annex at the center of the newly designated garden lawn area, which was previously farmland. This positioning aligns with the local character as it ensures the proposal is set back from the road and behind the existing main house. Additionally, the perimeter planting, once fully grown, will greatly obscure the building, enhancing privacy and security. Moreover, this location allows the client to create an inviting and attractive landscape surrounding the property.

Furthermore, this setting effectively captures the essence of a modern barn conversion, which is suited to the rural nature of the area. It is highly beneficial to have the new accommodation separate from the existing building, as it helps to preserve the scale of the existing structure and maintain the overall character of the area. Combining both buildings would have resulted in an overpowering presence that would dominate the surroundings.

The building is accessed from an extention of the block paved existing driveway in a path which avoids the existing trees root protection. The finishes would match the exisitng block paving to be coherant. The new connecting driveway will only be single car width which will naturally limit the use of the parking to the existing house, that it is intended to serve.

Access to the building is facilitated by extending the existing block paved driveway, ensuring that it avoids interfering with the protected roots of the existing trees. To maintain visual coherence, the finishes of the new driveway will be matched with the existing block paving. 

To manage parking requirements, the new connecting driveway will be designed as a single car width. This intentional choice serves to naturally limit parking usage to the existing house that it is intended to serve, thereby avoiding any potential overcrowding or congestion on the property.

The new proposal is located clear of the root protection zone, as is positioned to maximise the amount of south facing garden remaining.
The windows from the bedrooms will look South West over the front lawn. and planting keeping the main out look private and stimulating or the proposed tenant.
The area between the buildings will be planted simpathetically to engender an open aesthetic.

The new proposal has been carefully positioned to ensure it is clear of the root protection zone of the existing trees. This placement allows for the preservation of a significant portion of the south facing garden area. 

The bedrooms will offer windows that face the South West, providing pleasant views over the front lawn and surrounding planting. Through strategic landscaping choices, the main outlook from these windows will remain private and visually stimulating for the future tenant or occupant. 

Furthermore, the area between the existing building and the new annex will be planted in a manner that respects and enhances the open aesthetic of the site. These sympathetic plantings will contribute to the overall cohesive and attractive appearance of the grounds.

This scheme has been but forward for pre-planning application consideration and we look forward to receiving their recommendations.   
The scheme addressed our clients brief in a manor that we feel is in keeping with the local character and the size of plot.
The location of the building works for the existing tree keeping clear of root protection zone preserving the important scale of local planting.
The external appearance of the annex building and its material palette reference of farm barns, and are in harmony with that of the existing house. This is in consideration for the conservation area as it preserves the local character.

The proposed scheme has been submitted for pre-planning application consideration, and we eagerly anticipate receiving recommendations from the relevant authorities. 

We firmly believe that the scheme successfully addresses our client's brief while maintaining the local character and taking into account the size of the plot. Careful consideration has been given to the location of the building to ensure it remains clear of the root protection zone of the existing tree. This decision is crucial in preserving the important scale and integrity of the local planting.

The external appearance of the annex building, along with its chosen material palette, draws inspiration from the design of farm barns. This deliberate choice ensures harmony with the aesthetics of the existing house and reflects a sensitivity towards the conservation area. By staying true to the local character, the proposed design seeks to preserve the unique architectural heritage of the area.

The proposals are to address the clients need to provide dedicated care facilities for elderly family member as they prepare for their own retirement. 
The proposals consist of: 
- Two new flats, a 1 bed flat for frail elderly grandmother and a smaller studio flat for carer to provide 24 hour supervision. 
- New garage facilities are proposed on the ground floor which is sized to be appropriate for modern cars. With further room for garden storage.
-The flats are accessed from new stairs and platform lift.
-The new annex will be accessed via an extension of the existing drive. The width of the drive will only allow for single direction travel at one time, to maintain the maximum level of planted grounds around the new build. This measure works to tie the new development to the existing more permanently as there would be no value to the owner allowing access so close to their own front door.

The proposals have been developed to cater to the specific needs of the client, who requires dedicated care facilities for an elderly family member while also preparing for their own retirement. 

The core components of the proposals include:
- Two new flats: A 1-bedroom flat has been designed to accommodate the frail elderly grandmother, offering suitable amenities for her needs. Additionally, a smaller studio flat has been incorporated to provide 24-hour supervision from a dedicated carer.
- New garage facilities: The ground floor of the new annex will feature spacious garage facilities designed to accommodate modern cars. Ample space will also be allotted for garden storage requirements.
- Access and mobility considerations: Access to the flats will be facilitated through new stairs and a platform lift, ensuring ease of movement for both the elderly family member and the carer.
- Annex entry and driveway: The new annex will be accessed via an extension of the existing drive. Notably, the width of the drive will only allow for single-direction travel at a time in order to maintain a maximum level of planted grounds around the new build. This intentional measure aims to strengthen the integration of the new development with the existing property while acknowledging the value of keeping the access point closer to the owner's front door.

These proposals have carefully considered the specific requirements of the client and aim to provide a supportive and comfortable living environment for the elderly family member, promoting their well-being and maintaining a strong connection with the existing property.

The proposals are to address the clients need to provide dedicated care facilities for elderly family member as they prepare for their own retirement. 
The proposals consist of: 
- Two new flats, a 1 bed flat for frail elderly grandmother and a smaller studio flat for carer to provide 24 hour supervision. 
- New garage facilities are proposed on the ground floor which is sized to be appropriate for modern cars. With further room for garden storage.
-The flats are accessed from new stairs and platform lift.
-The new annex will be accessed via an extension of the existing drive. The width of the drive will only allow for single direction travel at one time, to maintain the maximum level of planted grounds around the new build. This measure works to tie the new development to the existing more permanently as there would be no value to the owner allowing access so close to their own front door.

The proposed scheme aims to fulfill the client's need for dedicated care facilities as they prepare for their own retirement, specifically catering to an elderly family member. The key components of the proposals are as follows:

1. Two new flats: The design includes a 1-bedroom flat specifically tailored to meet the needs of the frail elderly grandmother. Additionally, a smaller studio flat has been incorporated to ensure 24-hour supervision by a dedicated carer.

2. New garage facilities: The ground floor of the new annex will feature appropriately sized garage facilities to accommodate modern cars. Furthermore, sufficient space will be allocated for garden storage requirements.

3. Accessible entry: Access to the flats will be facilitated through the installation of new stairs and a platform lift, ensuring ease of movement for both the elderly family member and the carer.

4. Annex access via an extended drive: The new annex will be accessed through an extension of the existing drive. It is important to note that the width of the drive will only allow for single-direction travel at a time, preserving the maximum level of planted grounds around the new build. This measure serves to create a stronger and more permanent connection between the new development and the existing property. Additionally, the owner, recognizing the close proximity of the access point to their own front door, understands the limited value of allowing access to others in this area.

These proposals are carefully designed to address the specific care needs of the client's elderly family member while accommodating their own retirement plans. The aim is to provide a supportive and comfortable living environment, fostering well-being and integration within the existing property.
